title: Get the plot right first: Iris Murdoch on writing fiction
text:
I I'm not really a philosophical
novelist I mean I don't want the
philosophy to come in except in a very
background and roundabout sense perhaps
in relation to certain kinds of moral
problem but oh yes I mean I'm a
realistic writer I want to write about
life and life is very terrible and very
funny I mean people often ask me well I
want to write novels how do I do it and
I I think I do offer this advice that I
think it's very important to get the
structure right first to get the plot
right and you'll be rewarded enormously
by having the plot right there's also
the whole matter of the of the texture
of the work of the
funniness uh which I think should be
everywhere in the novel pure tragedy I
think belongs to the theater the the
novel is essentially tragic comic I
think I'm interested in the ambiguity of
the religious life uh that uh what what
is goodness what is virtue how does what
one attempt to become good how does one
give up the world what's it like to try
and um aim at virtue or aim at Holiness
and uh how does this go wrong in so many
ways I have no dogmatic religious belief
uh I don't believe in in in God or life
after death but I I I'm interested in
the phenomena of religion and I'm also
interested in the practice of of
religion and what what it's like but
this is very much also and is an aspect
of beinged morality and moral conflict
and moral failure and so on when I'm
inventing a book I spend a great deal of
time inventing the story possibly even
more time than I spend writing it I mean
that I I attach great importance to the
detailed invention of the plot and the
detail advention of the character so
that I can see the whole thing before I
start to write the first sentence I
don't find any difficulty in in writing
about men I hope that they my male
characters or convincing I I in my first
novel which was written with the male
narrator on the net uh one of my friends
did comment he said uh the fellow never
shaves you know they're all
[Music]
pitfalls
